description: Practice building responsive layouts with Flexbox, Grid, and modern CSS

challenges.title: Holy Grail Blog Layout
challenges.description: Build the classic holy grail layout using CSS Grid with named template areas. Requirements: (1) header spanning full width, (2) left sidebar for navigation, (3) main content area, (4) right sidebar for ads/widgets, (5) footer spanning full width, (6) the middle section should fill remaining viewport height.
hints: Use grid-template-areas with 3 columns and 3 rows
Use 1fr for the middle row and combine columns with repeated area names
Each child element needs grid-area set to the matching name
challenges.solution: .blog-layout { display: grid; grid-template-areas: 'header header header' 'nav main aside' 'footer footer footer'; grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr 200px; grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto; min-height: 100vh; gap: 4px; } header { grid-area: header; background: #333; color: white; padding: 20px; } nav { grid-area: nav; background: #444; padding: 20px; } main { grid-area: main; background: #555; padding: 20px; } aside { grid-area: aside; background: #444; padding: 20px; } footer { grid-area: footer; background: #333; color: white; padding: 20px; }

challenges.title: Responsive Card Gallery with Hybrid Layout
challenges.description: Build a responsive product card gallery combining CSS Grid and Flexbox. Requirements: (1) Grid-based card container with auto-fill/minmax for responsiveness, (2) each card uses Flexbox for internal layout (image, title, description, price, button), (3) cards must be equal height regardless of content length, (4) the button should stick to the bottom of each card, (5) smooth hover effect on cards, (6) no media queries should be used for the layout.
hints: Use repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr)) for the grid
Card uses display: flex; flex-direction: column;
The content area uses flex: 1 to push the button down
margin-top: auto on the button element
Intrinsic sizing handles responsiveness without media queries
challenges.starterCode:
Product Name

Description text here

$29.99
challenges.solution: .product-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr)); gap: 24px; padding: 24px; } .product-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: #fff; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .product-card:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); } .product-image { height: 200px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea, #764ba2); } .product-info { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; padding: 16px; flex: 1; } .product-info p { flex: 1; } .product-info button { margin-top: auto; background: #667eea; color: white; border: none; padding: 10px; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: 700; } .product-price { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700; color: #333; }

challenges.title: Component Library with Container Queries
challenges.description: Build a reusable UI card component library that responds to its container size using container queries (not media queries). Requirements: (1) Create a card component with image, title, description, and action button, (2) define the card as a container with container-type: inline-size, (3) at narrow widths (< 400px): image on top, full-width, content below, (4) at wider widths (>= 400px): image on left, content on right (side-by-side), (5) create a demo page that shows the same card component in different-sized containers (narrow sidebar, medium content, full-width hero), (6) each card automatically adapts to its container without knowing the viewport size.
hints: Set container-type: inline-size on each card wrapper
Use @container (min-width: 400px) query for the side-by-side layout
The img defaults to width: 100% in narrow mode
In wide mode, use flex or grid to put image + content side by side
Test by placing the same card in containers of different widths
challenges.starterCode:
Card Title

This card adapts to its container's width using container queries.
challenges.solution: .card-component { container-type: inline-size; container-name: card; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 12px; background: #fff; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .card-component img { width: 100%; border-radius: 8px; object-fit: cover; } @container card (min-width: 400px) { .card-component { flex-direction: row; align-items: center; } .card-component img { width: 200px; height: 150px; } .card-content { flex: 1; } } @container card (max-width: 399px) { .card-component { flex-direction: column; } .card-component img { width: 100%; height: 200px; } } .sidebar-container { width: 280px; } .main-container { width: 600px; } .hero-container { width: 100%; max-width: 900px; }
